content.problemStatement:
In a contest where NNN **new** users visited the contest,


AAA users just saw the problems and didn’t make any submissions and hence won’t get any rating.
BBB users who made a submission but could not solve any problem correctly. Thus, after the contest, they will get a rating in the range 800−1000800 - 1000800−1000.
Everyone else could correctly solve at least 111 problem. Thus, they will get a rating **strictly greater** than 100010001000 after the contest.
You need to output the number of new users in the contest who, after the contest, will get a rating and also the number of new users who will get a rating **strictly greater** than 100010001000.

The code given in the IDE uses the concept of **functions**. 

Update the code to solve the problem.


Input Format

Each input file contains of a single line, with three integers N,AN, AN,A and BBB - the number of new users, the number of users who just saw the problem and didn't make any submission, and the number of users who made a submission but could not solve any problem correctly.


Output Format
Output two integers separated by a space in a single line - the number of new users who will get a rating at the end of the contest and the number of new users who will get a rating higher than 100010001000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
There were 101010 new users. Among those 101010, 333 didn't make any submissions. This means that the other 10−3=710 - 3 = 710−3=7 users made submissions and will get a rating. This is the first integer of the output.
Now, among these 777 users, 222 couldn't solve any problem even though they submitted and tried. So, they will get a rating less than equal to 100010001000. The other 7−2=57 - 2 = 57−2=5 users were able to solve at least 111 problem and hence will get a rating greater than 100010001000. Hence, the second integer of the output is 555.

Explanation:
There were 101010 new users. Among those 101010, 444 didn't make any submissions. This means that the other 10−4=610 - 4 = 610−4=6 users made submissions and will get a rating. This is the first integer of the output.
Now, among these 666 users, 111 couldn't solve any problem even though they submitted and tried. So, they will get a rating less than equal to 100010001000. The other 6−1=56 - 1 = 56−1=5 users were able to solve at least 111 problem and hence will get a rating greater than 100010001000. Hence, the second integer of the output is 555.

Explanation:
There were 202020 new users. Among those 202020, 111 didn't make any submissions. This means that the other 20−1=1920 - 1 = 1920−1=19 users made submissions and will get a rating. This is the first integer of the output.
Now, among these 191919 users, 222 couldn't solve any problem even though they submitted and tried. So, they will get a rating less than equal to 100010001000. The other 19−2=1719 - 2 = 1719−2=17 users were able to solve at least 111 problem and hence will get a rating greater than 100010001000. Hence, the second integer of the output is 171717.

Explanation:
There were 100010001000 new users. Among those 100010001000, 300300300 didn't make any submissions. This means that the other 1000−300=7001000 - 300 = 7001000−300=700 users made submissions and will get a rating. This is the first integer of the output.
Now, among these 700700700 users, 700700700 couldn't solve any problem even though they submitted and tried. So, they will get a rating less than equal to 100010001000. The other 700−700=0700 - 700 = 0700−700=0 users were able to solve at least 111 problem and hence will get a rating greater than 100010001000. Hence, the second integer of the output is 000.
